title: bromopsis pumpelliana subsp. arctica
intro: Bromopsis pumpelliana subsp. arctica, also known as Arctic Bromegrass, is a sub-species of the Bromopsis pumpelliana plant, native to the Arctic tundra regions of North America and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Bromopsis pumpelliana subsp. arctica include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing antimicrobial protection. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
